2605.10067.title: Metis: Learning to Jailbreak LLMs via Self-Evolving Metacognitive Policy Optimization
2605.10067.paper_summary: Red teaming is critical for uncovering vulnerabilities in Large Language Models (LLMs). While automated methods have improved scalability, existing approaches often rely on static heuristics or stochastic search, rendering them brittle against advanced safety alignment. To address this, we introduce Metis, a framework that reformulates jailbreaking as inference-time policy optimization within an adversarial Partially Observable Markov Decision Process (POMDP). Metis employs a self-evolving metacognitive loop to perform causal diagnosis of a target's defense logic and leverages structured feedback as a semantic gradient to refine its policy, offering enhanced interpretability through transparent reasoning traces. Extensive evaluations across 10 diverse models demonstrate that Metis achieves the strongest average Attack Success Rate (ASR) among compared methods at 89.2%, maintaining high efficacy on resilient frontier models (e.g., 76.0% on O1 and 78.0% on GPT-5-chat) where traditional baselines exhibit substantial performance degradation. By replacing redundant exploration with directed optimization, Metis reduces token costs by an average of 8.2x and up to 11.4x. Our analysis reveals that current defenses remain vulnerable to internally-steered, closed-loop reasoning trajectories under the tested settings, highlighting a critical need for next-generation defenses capable of reasoning about safety dynamically during inference.
2605.10067.paper_summary_zh: 紅隊測試對於揭示大型語言模型（LLMs）中的漏洞至關重要。雖然自動化方法提高了可擴展性，但現有的方法往往依賴靜態啟發式或隨機搜索，使其在面對先進的安全對齊時變得脆弱。為了解決這個問題，我們引入了Metis，一個將越獄重新定義為對抗性部分可觀察馬爾可夫決策過程（POMDP）中的推理時間政策優化的框架。Metis採用自我演變的元認知循環來對目標的防禦邏輯進行因果診斷，並利用結構化反饋作為語義梯度來精煉其政策，通過透明的推理痕跡提供增強的可解釋性。對10個不同模型的廣泛評估表明，Metis在比較方法中達到了89.2%的最高平均攻擊成功率（ASR），在韌性前沿模型上（例如，O1的76.0%和GPT-5-chat的78.0%）保持高效能，而傳統基準則顯示出顯著的性能下降。通過用定向優化取代冗餘探索，Metis將令牌成本平均降低了8.2倍，最高可達11.4倍。我們的分析顯示，當前的防禦在測試設置下仍然容易受到內部驅動的閉環推理軌跡的攻擊，突顯出對於下一代防禦系統的迫切需求，這些系統能夠在推理過程中動態地考慮安全性。
2605.10067.author: Huilin Zhou et.al.
2605.10067.authors: Huilin Zhou, Jian Zhao, Yilu Zhong, Zhen Liang, Xiuyuan Chen, Yuchen Yuan, Tianle Zhang, Chi Zhang, Lan Zhang, Xuelong Li
